title: SWG Bypass Attempt Detection
spl:
index=proxy sourcetype="zscaler:web" earliest=-24h
| where match(lower(reason),"(?i)bypass|tunnel|direct|pac") OR match(lower(url),"(?i)proxy\.pac")
| stats count by user, src, reason
| sort -count
description: Detects attempts to reach direct IPs, misuse PAC files, or tunnel out of SWG inspection.
value: Security teams detect SWG bypass attempts by comparing active users against SWG-proxied traffic and monitoring for direct-to-internet connections from managed endpoints, ensuring all web traffic passes through cloud security inspection.
implementation: Correlate with firewall deny for non-standard ports. Tune for false positives from dev tools.
visualization: Table (bypass attempts), Bar chart (by user), Timeline.
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication
  by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src span=1h
| sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Anomalies from new SaaS app rollouts, browser updates, or vendor URL category changes.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Secure Web Gateway (SWG) logs from Zscaler ZIA, Netskope, or cloud proxy. Data in `index=zscaler` or `index=proxy` with `sourcetype=zscaler:zia:web`. Also: endpoint DNS logs or firewall logs to detect direct-to-internet connections. Key fields: `action`, `url`, `user`, `department`, `tunnelType` (ZscalerClientConnector/PAC/GRE), `proxyAction`.
• SWG bypass = any traffic that reaches the internet without going through the SWG. Methods: disabling the ZTNA/SWG client, using a personal hotspot, connecting via a non-inspected path, or using DNS-over-HTTPS to bypass DNS filtering.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Verify SWG coverage:
```spl
index=zscaler sourcetype="zscaler:zia:web" earliest=-24h
| stats dc(user) as users_through_swg
```
Compare with total active users from AD/IdP.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Primary search — Bypass detection via missing SWG traffic:
```spl
| inputlookup active_users.csv
| lookup swg_seen_users.csv user OUTPUT last_seen
| eval hours_since_seen=round((now() - strptime(last_seen, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S")) / 3600, 0)
| where hours_since_seen > 24 OR isnull(last_seen)
| table user, department, hours_since_seen
| sort - hours_since_seen
```

Understanding this SPL: Compares the list of active users (from AD) with users seen in SWG logs. Users not seen through the SWG in 24+ hours either have a disabled SWG client, are bypassing the proxy, or are not working. Cross-referencing with VPN/ZTNA login times helps distinguish.

Direct-to-internet detection (from firewall):
```spl
index=firewall earliest=-24h
| where dest_port IN (80, 443) AND NOT match(dest_ip, "^(10\.|172\.(1[6-9]|2[0-9]|3[0-1])\.|192\.168\.)")
| lookup swg_proxy_ips.csv dest_ip OUTPUT is_swg_ip
| where isnull(is_swg_ip)
| stats count dc(dest_ip) as unique_destinations by src_ip
| where count > 10
| sort - count
```

Step 3 — Validate
(a) Disconnect the SWG client on a test device. Verify the user disappears from SWG logs.
(b) Make a direct-to-internet connection. Verify the firewall detects it.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard: Row 1 — SWG coverage % of active users. Row 2 — Users missing from SWG. Row 3 — Direct-to-internet detections.
Alert: When SWG coverage drops below 95%. Immediate on direct-to-internet for managed devices.

Runbook:
1. **User missing from SWG** — Check if SWG client is installed and running. Push via MDM.
2. **Direct-to-internet** — Block at firewall. Require all HTTP/S through SWG.
3. **Coverage declining** — May indicate users disabling the client. Enforce via MDM policy.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **Cannot detect bypasses** — Firewall must log outbound HTTP/S connections. If cloud-only, use DNS monitoring.
• **`active_users.csv` stale** — Schedule a daily export from AD/IdP.
grandmaExplanation: All our internet traffic is supposed to go through a security filter in the cloud. We watch for employees who find ways around this filter — like turning off their security software or using a personal hotspot — because unfiltered internet access is how malware and data theft happen.
